Meet HII's Mission Technologies Division
- Our team of more than 7,000 professionals worldwide delivers all-domain expertise and advanced technologies in service of mission partners across the globe. Mission Technologies is leading the next evolution of national defense - the data evolution - by accelerating a breadth of national security solutions for government and commercial customers. Our capabilities range from C5ISR, AI and Big Data, cyber operations and synthetic training environments to fleet sustainment, environmental remediation and the largest family of unmanned underwater vehicles in every class. Find the role that's right for you. Apply today. We look forward to meeting you.
...
HII-Mission Technologies is seeking a Senior Generative AI Engineer to join the team. This will be a remote position, with occasional travel to the team's office in Annapolis Junction, MD. This role will support the team in creating and managing end-to-end generative AI solutions. The team supports AI and digital modernization for both internal use and for external clients, with applications from intelligence analysis to manufacturing optimization.

results.title: AI Engineer Intern | Careerport
highlights: an AI Engineer Intern, you'll work directly with business stakeholders, scientists, and engineers to design and ship AI-powered tools that improve how we operate, communicate, and make decisions across the mining and processing business unit. This is not a research role. You'll be building things that go into production and get used by real people on real projects. If you've shipped something with LLMs, agents, or data pipelines and you're curious about what those tools look like when applied to hard physical-world problems, this role was designed for you. Key Responsibilities: Design and build LLM-powered agents that automate and augment business workflows, ranging from document handling and stakeholder communication to operational Q&A and data retrieval Develop conversational interfaces over structured and unstructured data, enabling non-technical users to query operational and business data in plain English Integrate external data sources (commodity markets, regulatory filings, industry benchmarks) into internal tools and reporting workflows Collaborate with SMEs (engineers, metallurgists, and project managers) to translate domain knowledge into agent behavior, prompts, and retrieval pipelines Contribute to a shared Python codebase, including object-oriented modules for process modeling and data transformation Participate in sprint reviews and stakeholder demos, iterating rapidly based on feedback from end users Knowledge, Skills &Abilities: Minimum Requirements: Currently enrolled in a BS (junior or senior standing) or MS program in Computer Science, Data Science, Software Engineering, Applied Mathematics, or a related field Demonstrated experience shipping an AI/ML product or tool — portfolio, GitHub, or prior internship evidence required Proficiency in Python, including working with APIs, data structures, and third-party libraries Hands-on experience with at least one LLM framework or toolchain (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex, OpenAI/Anthropic APIs, Haystack) Comfort working with structured data (SQL or Pandas) and unstructured data (PDFs, text corpora) Strong written communication: you'll be writing prompts, specs, and documentation alongside code Nice to Have: Currently working on a PhD in Computer Science, AI, Data Science, Software Engineering, or similar Experience with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, ChromaDB), or embedding pipelines Familiarity with industrial, scientific, or time-series data (SCADA, historians, lab reports, assay data) Background or coursework in a physical science or engineering discipline (e.g. mining, metallurgy, chemical engineering, geology, or similar) Experience building multi-step or tool-using agents Exposure to BI tools or data visualization (PowerBI, Streamlit) Prior internship or project work in an industrial, energy, or materials company Physical Demands The work environment will be a mix of a typical office environment, a manufacturing floor and a construction site. Noise level: Normal office, manufacturing and construction noises. The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by people assigned to this job. They are not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, skills required of personnel so classified. highlights:

...
Product & Technology · Basel Office - MDPI Headquarters
...
# Data Scientist (100%)
...
## Drive innovation in machine learning and AI, from research to deployment, creating scalable intelligent systems that power the next generation of scientific discovery.
...
Are you passionate about turning complex data into intelligent, production-ready solutions while supporting the future of open-access science? We are looking for a Data Scientist to join our team and help design, develop, and deploy advanced machine learning solutions that power intelligent, data-driven products. This role focuses on NLP, recommender systems, and agentic LLM workflows, turning complex business challenges into scalable and impactful analytical solutions.
...
This is an opportunity to work at the forefront of machine learning and AI, contributing to innovative systems that leverage state-of-the-art research in practical applications.
...
Core Responsibilities
...
Design, develop, and evaluate machine learning and statistical models, with a focus on NLP, recommender systems, and LLM-based solutions.
...
Translate business problems into data-driven approaches and scalable analytical solutions.
...
Perform data exploration, preprocessing, and feature engineering to ensure high-quality inputs for modeling.
...
Develop, optimize, and benchmark models using appropriate metrics and validation strategies.
...
Build and experiment with LLM-powered systems, including agentic workflows and RAG.
...
Investigate, reproduce, and compare research prototypes and state-of-the-art methods.
...
Collaborate with engineering teams to support the integration of models into production.
...
Monitor and analyze model performance and iterate to improve accuracy and robustness.
...
Bachelor’s degree/ Master's degree in Computer Science or related.
...
2-5 years of experience as a Data Scientist.
...
2–5 years of experience in Python (including complex applications), Machine Learning, and LLMs.
...
Strong background in data science, statistics, and analytical problem-solving.
...
Intermediate proficiency in FastAPI, Celery, and Keycloak.
...
Intermediate proficiency in PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, and Hugging Face.
...
Proficiency in Natural Language Processing (NLP), including tokenization and named entity recognition (NER).
...
In-depth understanding of Artificial Intelligence principles.
...
Strong working knowledge of Microsoft O365 tools.

results.title: Senior Data Scientist at General Dynamics Information Technology (GDIT) | Jeremy
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist at General Dynamics Information Technology (GDIT) | Jeremy
...
Senior Data Scientist at General Dynamics Information Technology (GDIT) | Jeremy
...
[General Dynamics Information Technology (GDIT)gd.com](https://tryjeremy.com/companies/general-dynamics-information-technology)# Senior Data Scientist
...
Senior ML practitioner role designing and deploying production machine learning models for USCENTCOM's Data Office, working with on-premises GPU infrastructure and classified environments to solve operational intelligence problems across forecasting, NLP, anomaly detection, and computer vision domains.
...
## Job description
...
AI Frameworks, Applied Problem Solving, Data Compliance, Machine Learning Model Development
...
We are seeking a **Senior Data Scientist** to design, train, evaluate, and deliver machine learning models that solve operational problems across USCENTCOM’s Data Office initiatives. This is a hands-on ML practitioner role—not a platform or infrastructure position. The Senior Data Scientist will work within an established on-premises Data Analytical Environment (DAE) built on a Data Lakehouse architecture with H100 GPU infrastructure, applying their expertise in statistical modeling, deep learning, and applied ML to turn enterprise data into actionable intelligence. The ideal candidate brings deep experience in model development across multiple problem domains—forecasting, NLP, anomaly detection, and classification—and can independently lead the ML practice for the team.
...
* **Design, train, and validate **supervised, unsupervised, and deep learning models using open-source libraries (PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM) to support forecasting, classification, anomaly detection, and NLP use cases
...
* **Fine-tune and adapt open-source LLMs **(LLMA, Mistral, and similar) for domain-specific tasks including document summarization, entity extraction, and question-answering over classified and unclassified networks
...
* **Develop and maintain RAG pipelines: **chunking strategies, embedding model selection, retrieval evaluation, and prompt engineering to deliver high-quality LLM-augmented analytics
...
* **Translate mission requirements into ML solutions: **work directly with analysts, operators, and leadership to scope problems, define success criteria, and deliver models that produce actionable operational insights
...
* **Build models across multiple domains **including predictive analytics (logistics, readiness), NLP/text analytics (reports, intelligence documents), anomaly detection (cybersecurity, network, behavioral), and computer vision where applicable
...
* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Statistics, Applied Mathematics, Data Science, or related quantitative field
...
* 8+ years of hands-on AI/ML model development experience with a strong record of delivering production models, not just prototypes
...
* Strong Python proficiency and deep experience with open-source ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM, Hugging Face Transformers)
...
* Demonstrated ability to train, fine-tune, and evaluate models end-to-end—from raw data through feature engineering, model selection, training, validation, and production handoff
...
* Experience with LLM fine-tuning techniques (LoRA, QLoRA, PEFT) and RAG architecture design (vector databases, embedding strategies, retrieval evaluation)
...
* Working knowledge of MLOps toolchains (MLflow, DVC, Weights & Biases) and version control (Git).
...
* Experience with model optimization and conversion (ONNX, TensorRT, OpenVINO) for edge or tactical deployment
...
* Knowledge of NLP techniques applied to defense or intelligence domains (entity extraction, document classification, summarization of operational reports)
...
* Familiarity with distributed data frameworks (Apache Spark, Dask)
...
* Experience with edge AI hardware (NVIDIA Jetson, Coral TPU)

# Applied Data Scientist
...
Are you passionate about using data science, artificial intelligence, and advanced analytics to propel decisions? If you are interested in driving meaningful change for a growth focused consumer products brand, this is an opportunity to join the market-leading Delta Faucet Company team as we use analytics to turbocharge strategic insights!
...
Reporting to the Manager of Data Science and Analytics, this role will work with analytics and domain specialists across the business to deliver high-impact insights and data science solutions. You will develop advanced analytical models, experimentation tests, applied AI techniques, and partner with business stakeholders to translate complex data into clear, actionable recommendations that support the company’s growth initiatives.
...
As an Applied Data Scientist within Delta Faucet Company’s Analytics Center of Excellence (ACoE), you will act as an owner of high impact analysis and data science initiatives that support complex, ambiguous business decisions. This role focuses on advanced analytics, experimentation, and applied AI.

# Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience
...
Distinguished individual contributor role leading next-generation agentic AI systems and autonomous agents at enterprise scale across Walmart's global operations, combining deep ML/AI expertise with cross-functional mentorship and high-impact productionized solutions.
...
We are seeking a Distinguished Data Scientist to provide technical leadership and long‑term vision for next‑generation AI systems and platforms. This senior individual contributor will drive breakthrough system design and advanced solution development that transforms Walmart at scale. The role combines deep technical expertise (ML, simulation, optimization, agentic AI) with cross‑functional influence, mentoring of senior talent, and delivery of high‑impact, productionized solutions. You will be a great fit if: - You architect end-to-end ML/AI systems — from feature engineering through production deployment and monitoring - You’ve built and shipped multiple autonomous AI agents (not just experimented with LLM APIs) - You’re Level 5+ on Steve Yegge’s Vibe Coding scale and can ship production systems through AI-assisted development - You combine deep statistical rigor with practical business impact
...
About the Team The Associate AI Experiences team applies advanced data science, machine learning, and agentic AI to solve high-impact business problems across Walmart globally. You’ll build production Agentic systems, deploy autonomous AI agents, and translate complex data into actionable insights that drive measurable business value at massive scale. Our team builds intelligent platforms, agents and ML-powered solutions that make associates’ jobs easier, faster, and more fulfilling across stores, fulfillment centers, distribution centers, and home office. We leverage the Element LLM Gateway to safely access state-of-the-art language models while keeping all data within Walmart’s network. Our work spans NLP, computer vision, time series forecasting, and multi-agent AI systems at enterprise scale.

...

...
- Hands-on experience building and orchestrating multiple AI agents with LangChain, RAG, tool integration, and memory management.
...
- Multi-cloud ML platform proficiency — AWS SageMaker AND GCP (Vertex AI, BigQuery, BigQuery ML).

- Strong Python skills (Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn) with containerization (Docker) and MLOps practices.

About our group:  In this highly visible role, you will support the analysis and development of AI-driven solutions across Finance and Supply Chain functions. Leveraging low-code/no-code platforms, you will help build and deploy AI agents that integrate with existing enterprise systems, enhancing accessibility, usability, and overall operational efficiency. About the role - you will:  Collaborate closely with Finance and Supply Chain business and IT stakeholders to identify high-impact AI use cases Design and develop AI agents using low-code/no-code platforms such as Copilot Studio and n8n Partner with AI business leads and IT AI champions to drive adoption of deployed AI solutions Enable seamless integration of large language models (LLMs) with existing enterprise platforms Build strong cross-functional relationships to effectively work with diverse teams across the organization About you:  Excellent organizational skills with attention to detail and effective time management Excellent verbal and written communication skills Your experience includes:  Foundational understanding of generative AI tools, concepts, and techniques Familiarity with SQL and data querying Knowledge of finance and supply chain processes Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Business Administration, Finance, Supply Chain, or a related field, and returning to classes in Fall 2026. Location:  Remote United States Seagate is able to offer virtual employment for this position in the following states: AL, AZ, CO, CT, FL, GA, ID, IL, IN, KS, KY, MA, MD, MI, MN, MO, NC, NH, NJ, NM, NY, NV, OH, OK, OR, PA, SC, SD, TN, TX, UT, VA, WA, WI, WY. The estimated base wage range for this position is $22 - $30/hr. The individual base wage is based on work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Seagate offers comprehensive benefits to its eligible Interns, including, but not limited to, medical, dental, vision, and participation in the employee stock purchase plan. Seagate also offers Interns 12 paid holidays and a minimum of 48 hours of paid sick leave. The benefits for this position are based on a 24 to 40-hour-per-week schedule for a full calendar year and may differ depending on work location and tenure with the Company. Location: Remote United States Travel: None

# Data Scientist
...
Job Title: Data Scientist
...
Location: Monroeville, Pennsylvania
...
As a Data Scientist at BPMI, you will work under normal supervision and perform semi-complex critical technical functions to execute the BPMI mission in areas including, but not limited to applying data analysis, statistical modeling, and machine learning techniques to generate actionable insights that drive business decisions. This role involves collecting, analyzing, and interpreting both structured and unstructured data to support business solutions and operational improvements. Collaboration with cross-functional teams is key to identifying business challenges, developing predictive models, and communicating findings through reports, dashboards, and presentations. Continuous learning and adapting to emerging trends and technologies in data science are essential to fostering a data-driven culture within BPMI. The role ensures data accuracy, optimizes processes for efficiency, and works with stakeholders to enhance decision-making and business performance.
...
Essential Duties:
...
- Prepare, analyze, and interpret data from various sources using statistical analysis, predictive modeling, and computational techniques.
- Develop and maintain predictive models, analytical applications, and data pipelines to support business and engineering solutions.
- Collaborate with data owners to scope problems and implement data-driven solutions.
- Analyze both structured and unstructured data sets, ensuring statistical significance and accuracy in results.
...
- Present findings and insights in clear, accessible visuals such as reports, dashboards, and presentations.
...
- Contribute to knowledge sharing and documentation to promote learning within teams.
- Stay informed on emerging data science trends and technologies to improve methods and techniques.
- Apply data science methods to improve decision-making and operational efficiency.
...
- Participate in software development projects, including writing and testing code.
...
- Implement basic configuration management practices to track changes in code and data.
...
Minimum Job Requirements:
...
- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or related technical degree from an accredited college or university.
- A minimum of two (2) years of relevant experience.
- Ten (10) or more years of completed education and/or relevant experience may be considered to satisfy educational and years of experience requirements.
- Requires a security clearance; however, all qualified candidates will be considered regardless of their current clearance status. The ability to obtain and maintain a Department of Energy security clearance is required.
...
Preferred Job Requirements
...
- Minimum GPA of 3.0 is preferred for recent college graduates.
...
- Working knowledge of Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint. Outlook, etc.) and other commonly used business applications or role specific applications.
...
- Strong interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills –
...
- Ability to adapt to evolving project requirements while maintaining compliance with organizational policies, procedures, and applicable regulations. Strong desire to learn and adapt to new challenges.

results.title: Chief Engineer – Artificial Intelligence (AI) Applications | Jobflarely
highlights:
# Chief Engineer – Artificial Intelligence (AI) Applications
...
- General Dynamics Mission Systems (GDMS), a market leader and technology innovator, is seeking talented professionals to deliver cutting edge solutions to our DoD customers.GDMS has an immediate opening for the AI Applications Chief Engineer. Our team works closely with our DoD customers, Government laboratories, and industry partners to develop, deploy, and sustain military systems in defense of our nation, allies, and partners.The selected candidate will lead definition of AI-enabled technical strategies and solutions within our Maritime and Strategic Systems line of business. The mission demands we deliver advanced capabilities for our warfighters through high-reliability, affordable, cyber-resilient systems. As the AI Applications Chief Engineer, you will lead development of technology roadmaps and work closely with our business development organization to understand customer needs and present conceptual solutions aligned with GDMS capabilities. In this role, you will be responsible for leading the identification and cultivation of innovative AI initiatives, educating business and technical stakeholders about what AI can and cannot do, and working with senior engineering management to chart the course for growing our AI capabilities and workforce.In addition to receiving a competitive salary, flexible work environment, generous health and personal benefits, the successful candidate will enhance the skill set of a talented and technically accomplished group of colleagues. While this position can be fulfilled remotely, proximity to a GDMS office is desirable. Preferred locations include Scottsdale, AZ, Boston, MA, Pittsfield, MA, Bloomington, MN, Greensboro, NC, Pittsburgh, PA, and Manassas, VA.
...
- Preferred Qualifications:• Experienced AI architect with a robust background in Deep Learning (DL), advanced data visualization, software development, and full Machine Learning (ML) lifecycle management• Experience vetting potential use cases for AI capabilities, implementing viable approaches, and defining/tracking measures of success• Proficiency in ML and DL frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch) and understanding of validation methods for ML models• Proficiency in software development, with an emphasis on Python, and experience with distributed version control systems (e.g., Git, GitLab) and DevOps tools (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes)• Experience with the Linux command line including bash and shell scripting• Expertise in handling large, complex datasets including image, cyber, NLP, and signal data• Proven ability to engage and influence customers, understand requirements, present candidate solutions, and adapt solutions to align with evolving needs• Development and implementation of AI roadmaps in support of mission objectives, including planning and oversight of Independent Research and Development (IRAD) activities aligned with these objectives• Excellent oral and written communication skills including demonstrated ability to clearly present AI-enabled solutions to customer and company leadership, both technical and programmatic• Demonstrated success as technical lead on competitive white papers and proposals containing significant AI content• Experience assessing and managing technical risks and leading the investigation and resolution of high priority technical issues on critical, high visibility programs• Demonstrated success leading large, distributed, multi-disciplinary teams in developing and delivering cost-effective, AI-enabled solutions to customers• Working knowledge of AI-related AWS or Azure services• Familiarity with addressing regulatory compliance (e.g., cyber, legal) as part of deploying AI solutions
